channel.title: Multiplayer on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.
channel.description: Recent content in Multiplayer on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.

item.title: Single vs. Multiplayer RPG Designs
item.description:
Hi everybody. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about getting jiggy with it or, more specifically, the application of jiggy oriented procedures to a variety of activities In particular.

Wait, actually today I’m answering a question about single versus multiplayer RPG designs. This question comes from Games by Mark Wolf, who asked: “Here’s a question for you, Tim: What are the pros and cons of solo RPGs versus multiplayer RPGs? Is there a difference? Further discussion point. My answer to the second question is that I would argue a solo RPG, if it amounts to nothing else, is an incomplete design.

item.title: CounterBite: Bloodlines Multiplayer Design
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about counter bite, the Vampire: The Masquerade — Bloodlines multiplayer mode that we had suggested, that we had designed but never implemented for Vampire: The Masquerade — Bloodlines.

I’ve talked about it here and there in other videos. A brief recap — and remember I was not on Bloodlines team for the first two years — a brief recap — is part of the original design for Bloodlines that we were doing for Activision using valves engine from halflife was a multiplayer mode. It was written down, it was designed, but at some point when Activision was concerned about that — the single player game was really big and they wanted us to focus on it. They took multiplayer mode away from troa and gave it to another team — I don’t know if it was an in-house Activision team or another out of housee team. They made a whole new design, didn’t get very far with it I don’t know what happened. I don’t know if it was they weren’t working out or Activision just didn’t want to spend the. Money, but that whole thing got cancelled and bloodline shipped as a single player game. I did finally find our design document for what I always call counter Bite — part of the reason I had trouble finding it wasn’t called counter bite, it just says multiplayer. It’s chapter nine of the Vampire: The Masquerade — Bloodlines design document.

item.title: Why I Don't Play Online Anymore
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about why I don’t play online games much anymore, and I’m particularly talking about multiplayer, and I did it for a long time.

I mean, multiplayer games have been around for a long time. I’m not just talking about, you know, giant MMOs. Even the consoles that started coming out in the late ’70s, like the Atari BCS, it supported a lot of two-player games, and even a lot of four player games, and most of the consoles back then did They usually had two ports in the front, um or they, and frequently the second Port could have a tap that would allow multiple controllers. So you — that’s how you would play — two and four people games.

item.title: Storytelling in MMORPGs
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk to you about storytelling in massively multiplayer online RPGs.

I recently did a podcast — it was really good, should be up in August — and the interviewer uh, who was asking me all kinds of really good questions. I mean, I love when a journalist or anyone interviewing you asked you really evocative questions that make you think, rather than, what was your influence for this game? How did you design this area? What made you think of this feature? I mean, I get it. Maybe you’ve been told to ask those questions, but I’ve had answered those a billion times. So what he asked towards the end of the — uh, I don’t want to call an interview, we just had a discussion for a couple hours. He said: what got away from you? Because I know you’re retiring — what do you think got away from you?

item.title: MMO Stories
item.description:
Hey everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about why I got into MMOs, like why I even went to carbine in the first place to make an MMO, because a lot of people thought that this was a bit of a Divergence from what

I had done before, because I made mostly, at least since Fallout, since 94. I’d done nothing but RPGs. Well, I already talked in a previous video how much I really got into LP muds in grad school. I talked about darker Realms, snow cows and stunts. I pulled as a wizard. That, of course, got me into MMOs. When Troika started in 98, that’s when EverQuest came out and EverQuest was pretty much a mud and with Graphics thrown on the front end and I was having so much fun with that game I would go home and play it every night. I had dozens of characters on multiple servers.

item.title: Vampire Bloodlines - Cut Multiplayer Content
item.description:
Hi, it’s me Tim uh. Today I want to talk about vampire, the Masquerade Bloodlines, the Lost multiplayer content.

But first we got to mention two things. One: I’m trying to fix the technical issues. The note saw video glitching yesterday, a few days before that it was a Microsoft glitching, I’m sorry, my microphone glitching. I think I know what was causing those. Let’s find out, also, I got a ring light, although it’s Square, so I should be better lit now, and it really makes my eyes pop, which I know some of you can notice.
